# Test Companion: Leo

## Purpose
Define proof families that show Leo preserves emotional truth and realism.

## Goal to Prove
Leo identifies false pose and suggests realism-preserving edits.

## Current Active Slice
- False-pose diagnosis first: when a paragraph sounds impressive or emotionally loaded but not believable, Leo should name the realism or emotional-truth problem before rewriting.
- Boundary discipline: Leo should make the text feel more human without collapsing into generic simplification advice.
- Broad-round rule: each active slice should be run across multiple source roles so the slice is judged on source-role variation, not one lane alone.

## Minimum Proof
- One non-leading run where Leo identifies the false-pose or realism problem before proposing edits.
- One boundary run where Leo improves human truth without reducing the answer to generic "make it simpler" advice.
- Broad coverage across multiple source-role lanes before the slice is treated as stable.
